services.title: Scenic Tours
services.description: Guided botanical journeys through 60 acres of flora, historic ruins, and serene riverside trails.
services.details: Walk beneath towering palms, discover hidden riverside paths, and explore historic estate ruins with knowledgeable guides who bring Dominica's botanical heritage to life. Perfect for families, photographers, and nature lovers.

services.title: Weddings & Events
services.description: Host your special day amidst our lush, botanical paradise. A breathtaking backdrop for memories.
services.details: From intimate ceremonies beneath the canopy to grand celebrations on our landscaped grounds, Paradise Valley offers an unforgettable setting. Our team works with you to create a magical experience surrounded by tropical beauty.

services.title: Landscaping & Hardscaping
services.description: Expert consultation and design to transform your space into a tropical haven, featuring custom hardscapes.
services.details: Our landscape architects understand the Caribbean climate intimately. We design and build gardens, patios, pathways, and water features that thrive in Dominica's unique volcanic soil and tropical conditions.

services.title: Plant Variety
services.description: From rare Anthuriums to citrus and mango fruit trees, we stock plants tailored to the Caribbean climate.
services.details: Our nursery specializes in species that flourish in Dominica's climate — including the rare Anthurium dominicense, exotic tropicals, citrus trees, mango varieties, and endemic palms propagated on-site.

services.title: Gardening Supplies
services.description: High-quality soil, organic fertilizers, pots, and professional tools for every green thumb.
services.details: Everything you need to cultivate a thriving garden — premium volcanic soil blends, organic fertilizers formulated for tropical plants, quality pots, and professional-grade tools sourced for Caribbean gardeners.

services.title: Plant Care
services.description: Valuable resources and guidance to help you maintain healthy, vibrant plants.
services.details: Our horticultural experts share practical knowledge tailored to Dominica's climate — watering schedules, pest management, pruning techniques, and seasonal care tips to keep your garden flourishing year-round.

events.title: Paradise Valley Jazz & Arts 2027
events.description: An unforgettable celebration of live jazz, local art, and the natural beauty of Dominica's botanical heartland.
events.longDescription: Join us for a day of world-class jazz performances set against the stunning backdrop of our 60-acre botanical estate. Local artists showcase their work, food vendors serve island favorites, and the gardens come alive with music, culture, and community spirit.

hero.title: Cultivating Nature in Borne
hero.subtitle: A 60-acre sanctuary where Dominica's botanical heritage comes alive.

sections.title: A Living Masterpiece
sections.content: Welcome to Paradise Valley Garden & Nursery, a sanctuary rooted in the fertile volcanic soil of Borne, St. John. More than just a nursery, we are a destination where nature, history, and community converge on the Nature Island.
sections.imageAlt: Flowering archway framing the tropical gardens at Paradise Valley

sections.title: Heritage & Wildlife
sections.content: Home to vibrant resident peacocks, roaming donkeys, and historic estate artifacts, our grounds offer a glimpse into Dominica's rich heritage. Wander through pathways lined with endemic palms, discover riverside trails, and encounter the wildlife that calls our gardens home.
sections.imageAlt: Peacock atop a historic estate cannon among red blooms

sections.title: Committed to Conservation
sections.content: We are dedicated to sustainability, propagating rare Anthurium dominicense and exotic tropicals that thrive in our unique island climate. Every plant we grow supports local biodiversity and the Dominican economy.
sections.imageAlt: Ancient buttress-rooted tree on the Paradise Valley estate

values.title: Biodiversity
values.description: Protecting and propagating rare endemic species across our 60-acre estate.

values.title: Community
values.description: Supporting local artisans, gardeners, and the Dominican economy.

values.title: Education
values.description: Sharing knowledge about sustainable tropical gardening practices.

values.title: Experience
values.description: Creating unforgettable moments for visitors, couples, and families.

hero.title: Come Experience Paradise
hero.subtitle: We're open seven days a week in the heart of Borne, St. John.

visit.directions: Paradise Valley is located in Borne, St. John Parish, on Dominica's Nature Island. Follow signs from the main road — our entrance is well-marked. Allow approximately 30 minutes from Roseau.
tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes for garden tours
Bring sunscreen and water — Dominica's sun is strong
Cameras welcome — our grounds are photographer-friendly
Peacocks roam freely — please admire from a respectful distance
